title: Neomammillaria mollendorffiana
taxonomy: Neomammillaria mollendorffiana is classified as a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It is a type of mammillaria, which is a genus of small cacti. Mammillaria species are native to Mexico and Central America, and Neomammillaria mollendorffiana is specifically found in Mexico. Neomammillaria mollendorffiana is further categorized as a part of the subgenus Mammillaria subgenus Mammillaria subgenus Mammillaria  (not a full classification). The subgenus in question is not further classified.

intro: Neomammillaria mollendorffiana, also known as the Mammillaria mollendorffiana, is a species of flowering plant in the cactus family, characterized by its small, globular stems with short, fine spines and vibrant pink flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments such as wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Neomammillaria mollendorffiana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Neomammillaria mollendorffiana is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
